---
title: "International Students"
format: html
---

The MSDS program welcomes students from around the world and is committed to supporting the unique needs of international scholars during their academic journey.


## Visa and Immigration Information

The University’s [Office of International Affairs (OIA)](https://internationalaffairs.uchicago.edu/) provides guidance on:

- Obtaining and maintaining F-1 or J-1 visa status
- SEVIS requirements
- Travel documentation
- Optional Practical Training (OPT) and Curricular Practical Training (CPT) eligibility

Students are responsible for maintaining lawful immigration status throughout their studies.


## Cultural and Campus Resources

UChicago offers resources to help international students adjust to life in Chicago, including:

- Orientation programs and workshops
- Language and writing support
- Student cultural organizations and affinity groups
- International student mixers and networking events

Students are encouraged to engage with the broader UChicago community to enhance their academic and cultural experience.

## Full-Time Course Load and RCL exception

There is a [full-time course load requirement](https://internationalaffairs.uchicago.edu/students/current-students/maintaining-a-full-time-course-load) for international students on student visas, unless the student applied for a **Reduced-Course-Load (RCL)** exception. 

Among the different types of RCL, the **[Final Quarter RCL](https://internationalaffairs.uchicago.edu/students/current-students/maintaining-a-full-time-course-load#finalquarterrcl)** is the most common. Students should apply for RCL through OIA starting *around two weeks before the start of the quarter they plan to graduate*. You will need to download and fill out the Verification of Completion (VOC) form for the Director's signature as well.

## Optional Practical Training (OPT) and Curricular Practical Training (CPT)

International students on F-1 visas may be eligible for work authorization through Optional Practical Training (OPT) and Curricular Practical Training (CPT, course-based for MSDS). These opportunities allow students to gain hands-on experience related to their academic program. See sections on [OIA website](https://internationalaffairs.uchicago.edu/students/current-students) for current information.
